description: Log4Shell (Log4j JNDI RCE) attack preconditions catalog. Evidence-ranked conditions required for Log4Shell exploitation across five attack paths.

AP-L4.label: log4j_loaded_at_runtime
AP-L4.description: Log4j classes are loaded during execution.

AP-L7.label: outbound_egress_permits_callback
AP-L7.description: Network policy permits outbound connectivity needed for remote resolution/callback.

AP-L8.label: attacker-controlled_input_reaches_a_log4j_sink
AP-L8.description: Untrusted input is logged by Log4j on a reachable code path.

AP-L9.label: exposure_class_supports_attacker_access
AP-L9.description: Service exposure posture allows attacker interaction (public/internal/post-auth).

AP-L10.label: jndi_lookup_capability_present
AP-L10.description: Required lookup capability/classes are present/active in runtime.

AP-L11.label: lookups_enabled_in_effective_configuration
AP-L11.description: Effective logging configuration permits lookups / message substitution relevant to the path.

AP-L12.label: dns_resolution_allowed
AP-L12.description: Environment permits DNS resolution required for outbound target discovery.

AP-L13.label: ldap_rmi_outbound_permitted
AP-L13.description: Environment permits LDAP/RMI egress or equivalent protocol needed for classic JNDI retrieval.

AP-L14.label: http_https_outbound_permitted
AP-L14.description: Environment permits HTTP(S) egress (alternative retrieval / callback).

AP-L15.label: proxy-only_outbound_with_allowlist
AP-L15.description: Outbound is constrained to proxy and allowlist governs destinations.

AP-L16.label: process_spawn_permitted
AP-L16.description: Policy allows exec/spawn needed for high-impact payloads.

AP-L17.label: relevant_file_write_permitted
AP-L17.description: Policy allows writing to locations needed for persistence/impact.

AP-L18.label: outbound_channel_lacks_strong_endpoint_authentication
AP-L18.description: Outbound retrieval/callback channel can be intercepted/modified due to missing TLS validation, weak trust, or use of cleartext protocols.

AP-L19.label: no_certificate_pinning___proxy_interception_permitted
AP-L19.description: Client permits interception (e.g., enterprise proxy) and does not pin certificates or otherwise prevent MITM substitution.

log4j_mitm_injection_v1.description: Log4j Outbound Retrieval with MITM Injection v1
